title: `flushSync()` inside a render-phase effect can permanently kill the effect tree
author: Deeds67

title: `SvelteMap.set` on an existing key notifies `keys()` readers even though the key set didn't change
author: Nic-Polumeyv
body:
### Describe the bug

Setting a new value for a key that already exists in a `SvelteMap` re-runs effects that only read the key set (`keys()`, `has()` misses), as long as that key was never read individually. Such a key has no source yet, so `set()` takes the new-key branch and increments the version signal unconditionally even though the key set didn't change. In practice `{#each [...map.keys()] as key}` re-renders on every value write into the map.

Fix incoming.

### Reproduction

https://svelte.dev/playground#H4sIAAAAAAAAA22QwWrDMAyGX0WIQRLIXHbN0kAfoKcdkx5cV9lMHdnYarsS8u7DzUYvOwn96PskNCPribDBjys5ob0OcKZ7An-l-Mpe7GiNFusZaxRt3c3yCZtRu0Q1jtZRwqafUe4hS3KA9Z9yF4JKDy3WeNSJ_suNZyGWhA22yUQbpBt4EDsFHwVmeN61wBj9BMWKbiJpI_Zq5V68D5wZ4zkJTDrAFphuT7Ts-0IXNbwdDtXv7AuNIxkpywq2Hcw5WgXekXL-syzyG5qihl4pNemgcl9Wq0CWXNrN82BujxcRz-DZOGvO23k1ZzKRlI_9ey1fKmo--amsqqVLJKDbzUp2w-PH9C3YSLzQclh-AOX_npidAQAA

Click the button and watch the console, `keys:` logs on every click although the key set is always `['a']`.

### Severity

annoyance

title: $state.eager() is sometimes incorrect in SSR
author: adiguba

title: experimental.async: batch merging permanently swallows unrelated synchronous UI updates when an earlier async batch never settles
author: reinhard-sanz
body:
### Describe the bug

Since **5.55.6** (#18205, "blocking" → "merging" batch strategy), a batch that shares **any** written (non-derived) source with an earlier still-pending batch is merged into it (`#find_earlier_batch()` → `#merge()`). Two consequences:

1. If the earlier batch never settles (a hung promise — or just a slow one, for its duration), every merged batch's DOM updates are withheld, **including purely synchronous updates to unrelated parts of the UI**. No error, no warning, no pending UI. The `$state` values are updated (reads see the new values); only the DOM commit never happens.
2. `#merge()` copies the merged batch's `current` sources into the stuck batch, so the stuck batch's "contagion set" **grows with every merge**. Batches that share a source only with a *previously merged* batch — not with anything the stuck batch originally wrote — get swallowed too. One hung async can progressively deaden the whole app, interaction by interaction.

On **≤ 5.55.5** the same interaction sequence works: the blocking strategy only blocked a batch when it intersected *and* differed from the earlier batch's writes, and, crucially, transitively-connected batches did not block, because `#is_blocked()` consulted the blocker's own `blocking_pending`, which is empty for a batch that merely got deferred (it has no async of its own).

### Reproduction

[Broken (5.56.4)](https://svelte.dev/playground/untitled?version=5.56.4#H4sIAAAAAAAAE41SQW7bMBD8yoINEAl2oliNe1BkAT321ktPdQHT0lomIi8JchXXEATk2nuf0PZhfklB0bJTNCh6IcDd4czscDtBcociE9IdqIQd2hqBrTRiKjaqQSeyz53gg_EYXxDT8cV7Y27dEzbsa2vp8LV6qYmR2IlM5K60ynCxpCU3yMBW1TVaWMCVY8kY3cUPY8_NXi-nr5Ybucbm0rkmfEILrakkY3U94GjJYcJNSyUrTeAavY84hs73lqw2EDEUcBeD3EvFQLiHj1bvlMMoimFRQNfHD5AksJVUO9hoO8hoKhEs3myUxSpwWeTWEqyCokXXNgxXHferwXK_pDy5hEH59r4I0L22j9BShRYkrHVLlbSHPNneF0vKQ6bZWB5yzE3RBbthnBBp3OeJGfrdG0fKGGQwSJWiOor73BSNlv5yfP7lgV1yAgVjf8sMDstGlY_AW3QIikDbCu1obd0ya_JReNCiO-U1fvFk8gBu5s--H2zNYnAsLQ9RKqohjB_trWJ0582YgJvF3lPg_5dSoHfpC5E0hrVVVY1nXjfzlOn_Ug5k426tnlTJagcDCCuIXLq46lzax6uz4tsYAioDP8-NpuYAnz6cNvFiI4VJ4P3TScj5pKNbNi3D8fn76bkDTeA37hA8-E84fvsxv53Pb-dTeLnzDmQtFQXEzwHxbvwqU-SOraa66AYLfZ6c7mFnxFQwfmWRsW2x_9L_BqjI4AQhBAAA)

[Working (5.55.5)](https://svelte.dev/playground/untitled?version=5.55.5#H4sIAAAAAAAAE41SQW7bMBD8yoINEAl2oliNe1BkAT321ktPdQHT0lomIi8JchXXEATk2nuf0PZhfklB0bJTNCh6IcDd4czscDtBcociE9IdqIQd2hqBrTRiKjaqQSeyz53gg_EYXxDT8cV7Y27dEzbsa2vp8LV6qYmR2IlM5K60ynCxpCU3yMBW1TVaWMCVY8kY3cUPY8_NXi-nr5Ybucbm0rkmfEILrakkY3U94GjJYcJNSyUrTeAavY84hs73lqw2EDEUcBeD3EvFQLiHj1bvlMMoimFRQNfHD5AksJVUO9hoO8hoKhEs3myUxSpwWeTWEqyCokXXNgxXHferwXK_pDy5hEH59r4I0L22j9BShRYkrHVLlbSHPNneF0vKQ6bZWB5yzE3RBbthnBBp3OeJGfrdG0fKGGQwSJWiOor73BSNlv5yfP7lgV1yAgVjf8sMDstGlY_AW3QIikDbCu1obd0ya_JReNCiO-U1fvFk8gBu5s--H2zNYnAsLQ9RKqohjB_trWJ0582YgJvF3lPg_5dSoHfpC5E0hrVVVY1nXjfzlOn_Ug5k426tnlTJagcDCCuIXLq46lzax6uz4tsYAioDP8-NpuYAnz6cNvFiI4VJ4P3TScj5pKNbNi3D8fn76bkDTeA37hA8-E84fvsxv53Pb-dTeLnzDmQtFQXEzwHxbvwqU-SOraa66AYLfZ6c7mFnxFQwfmWRsW2x_9L_BqjI4AQhBAAA)

title: `flushSync()` errors when called at top level of script; doesn't work with `$effect.pre`
author: jacekalms
body:
### Describe the bug

In versions 5.53.12 and later. Works in 5.53.11.

Gives error:
> `null is not an object (evaluating 'batch.transfer_effects') in <unknown>`

Happens in async and non-async modes.

May be related to https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/issues/18209

### Reproduction

Simple reproduction in playground:

https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.53.12#H4sIAAAAAAAAE22PsQ6CQAyGX6V2gsRIXJGYuLk7eg4IJVxy9C53RSSEd_dAo4tb--X7_6YTctkR5ngmYywM1psaEqq1UJ3iFhttKGB-nVBGt3gLiPyTOjm3Cw8ysrB7GegfrywLscQaLELltZOjYiWKAXTnrBeYoDF9aC8jVzBD420HCt95hYcoR_1rJOlKiuzXxUW7P64fbIosjorjWaGnYC6-p_kWt1KbQXONeVOaQPMLi6-2o_kAAAA

Seems to be fixed if you add at least one `$effect` block:

https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.53.12#H4sIAAAAAAAAE22Puw7CMAxFf8VYDK2EQKylVGJjZyQMpXVEpNSJEpeHqv47KSBY2Oyjc_0YkOuOsMA9Wevg5oJtIaPWCLU5LlAbSxGL44Dy8JM3gcQ_qZ33y3glKxM715H-8caxEEsag2VsgvFSKVaiGMB03gWBAbTt4-Xw4AZG0MF1oPCdV7hJctK_RpZ_yJy0pkayLIdtBcP45uXqt4PLy7p6fTYrV6lUnM4RugsWEnoaT6mrjb0ZbrHQtY00PgFKuK8_EQEAAA

But not if you use an `$effect.pre`:

https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.53.12#H4sIAAAAAAAAE22Puw7CMAxFf8VYDK2EQKylVGJjZyQMpXVEpNSJEpeHqv47KSBY2Oyjc_0YkOuOsMA9Wevg5oJtIaPWCLU5LlAbSxGL44Dy8JM3gcQ_qZ33y3glKxM715H-8caxEEsag2VsgvFSKVaiGMB03gWBAbTt4-Xw4AZG0MF1oPCdV7hJctK_RpZ_yJy0pkayLIdtBcP45uXqt4PLy7p6fTYrV6lUnM4RugsWEnoaT6mrjb0ZbrHQtY00PgFKuK8_EQEAAA

And calling `flushSync()` if you have an `$effect.pre` block doesn’t actually run the effect, and then breaks future reactivity (also in 5.53.11):

https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.53.11#H4sIAAAAAAAAE21RTU_DMAz9K8baoRNoE9fSFiEu3DkSDl3qsIgsqRqXMVX97zjNBprEIYr9_D6sZELfHghLfCHnAhzD4DooqLNM3Rrv0FhHEcu3CfnUJ14CBD-rnvp-E7_IccJ2baT_cB08k2exwSrqwfbcKK9YeXvow8AwgXFj3L-evIYZzBAOoDCrFT4IVciOGCLUsIrcMhUKW4Xr82xFxpDmTT9QUayhbmBa_FmCY3C0ceFDFJlVijfcQlzEPF88krfCXc7j332Ky9yMXrMNHnYjc_DPzupP6iTt36gr0nlRziFy6hSlZY1HuWUMZe7zRimv2v69k6_2983yOzfVVkoBsj0Er1NCPV3Fzc1SnKpthkUgf8D0zVjyMNL8Ll1r3dH6DkvTukjzDxqYqxYGAgAA

Interestingly, `flushSync` works if it’s not called from the top level of the script, but instead e.g. with a button handler:

https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.56.4#H4sIAAAAAAAAE3VSXU-DMBT9K9dmDxANqI8MZtii8V0TH8QHhItr1rWE3jkXwn-35XMkWwihPT33nMPtrZlM98gC9opCKDiqSuTgYM4Jc5fdsYIL1Cz4rBmdSsuzgMH7qrgsPf2Lgiz2nWq8hGdKEkoyMizUWcVLWiUyIfvyfakqghoKcdDbt5PMoIGiUntIWFefsOVAFkhgtSolIIKFppTQSVicMHc5nOvp5L5FDb7AosCMvLJCx3EhWhk_o6OVQE-oHyPx_PLyvHnvdaybeazS6BZF0BrBEzxAAI_LgTXXAdCBIcIt6C5S447pi4PMiCsJ3wciJTeCZzvMTZ568PT9NhaBxONm_M9LGRK2Nt-g2y9n1VOYmY8Hb0jE5c-oR6pLOpm5M6Xefjo-641hfKhqpy93oecHsB5upuf0km36ER9v3jnv_hXF-JpifF2RGisb-tPoyXD7sGoH_ib0zdIAXbNAycz2K6pnzWtW7eIU-h1sCsxYE_4RC6g6YPNldikXRy5zFhSp0Nj8A4zhabtZAwAA

Or if you wrap the `flushSync()` call in `queueMicrotask`:

https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.56.4#H4sIAAAAAAAAE21RO0_DMBD-K8epQypQK9aQBCEWFiZGzJA6F2rVsYN9oVRR_juXB6CiDpbvPn-Ps92jKxvCFJ_IWg9HH2wFCVWGqVrjDdbGUsT0tUc-tSNvBARfVA9tu4mfZHnEdmWkS7j2jsmx2GAWdTAtF8opVs40rQ8MPdS2i_uXk9MwQB18AwpntcI7oQrZEkOEHFaRS6ZEYalwvZytqK5J86YNlCRryAvoJ3-W4Ogtbax_F8XMSsUbriFOYh5-PD466ujZ6OC5jIdzmzFX4W6eRfrfaZN_JnXnNBvvYNcxe_dojT5QJV4X5zkjLbdZ0mTlY6aWWe9ll2NI535OHPOy7d9jumx_W0xfeJVtpRRgtgfv9JiQ92dxQzEVp2w7wyKQj2L6Ykw5dDS8SVcaezSuwrQubaThG8vMaWIrAgAA

title: Keyed {#each} blocks fail when using dynamic keys for destructuring
author: SukeshP1995
body:
### Describe the bug Consider the following Child component ```svelte {#each options as { [labelKey]: label, [valueKey]: value } (value)}

{label}: {value}

{/each} ``` Consider the Main svelte file ```svelte ``` Throws the following error `Can't find variable: labelKey in in Child.svelte in App.svelte in __wrapper.svelte in undefined` **Notes** - Remove `(value)` in the `Child.svelte`, the code works. - If you move the keyed each block to Main svelte file the code works. ### Reproduction [svelte playground link](https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.56.4#H4sIAAAAAAAAE3WQ0W7CMAxFf8XKJhWkigp4yzqkaS-T9gmFh0CNiBSaqAllKMq_z2nTbkPjLT6xr32vZ404I-PsA5XScNWtqmGGtXRYz1nOjlKhZbzyzN1M7IuAeJp6M2ZhO1Qusr2w-B8_6MZh40iGlfbQSuM222br5Nno1sH7SdLKY6vPkC2KvkqjWexS6EAbJ3Vj4RUqD4LDMoeOQyaWGQTIoWerxFbEElontCa0G7WU2KP6xBuJZSJ7GXEn1AUT7iIui59Tm3I4Mjb7dEzoi1FtqEYRqgqaI-sOvxzjrr1gyB9E-Nvx3xDvfh7EGK_3k618cpJPsQUy9WxabexsfmfMP6E4nKZOYUmqGrV2fJDNoRpFCfVP0pz1j3nvvDSbIYrAYUghlIWhDb6I-uE-ix1VQqqrbGrGj0JZDN-Qg3REiQIAAA) ### Logs ```Shell ``` ### System Info ```Shell svelte playground ``` ### Severity annoyance ### Additional Information _No response_

title: {const and {let are not updating as expected
author: knrdl
body:
### Describe the bug {@const} is considered legacy and {const} should be used as replacement: * https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/@const * https://sveltejs.github.io/eslint-plugin-svelte/rules/no-at-const-tags/ However, {const} is not behaving as expected because it is not updating when the defining reactive expression changes its value. See repro below. ### Reproduction [Playground](https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.56.4#H4sIAAAAAAAAA61TXYvbMBD8K4vuIAmYGO7RtUNDS2nBUOjXSxyKLK_P4hRZSLLvgvF_L3Ikn8Plqdw-aUfjmVlZGoikJyQJ-YpCtPDcalHBGitusdqQiNRcoCHJYSD2rBzPASQKX-2V2poehXVYSQ3ewlkrLUprSEJSwzRXdldIAACBFr7k-z_ff__4m3_7-QsyOKxY2z5xNEBlBUwjPa0iWJ24tMCalgFruHKIpkaVqPUZNFdK4OpYyFfVWtC-7bSBDO6NpRbXh-Pmw7T_SsIe9fkzat5j5YjVZbleRtpOpLXXg2w3S2-5ZKKr0ITNzcY5pPE8YiGHO16D1R2OF9fhI2ul8c57-2lqMvhvQy-bNg-7pWQ2LLsxjZsHf-LDwv_d3Zfeb53nE8_xXV1zDJ45XjnODP-LPct3gem5ahcssyGsxjRWFy0_wh1S1lzfWGpCVp9rUhO0ROFjzCCXqrPXmCv3rrKCsAbZU9m-FOQtxT2qrCAh1y1KT0WHc_RFllAll1XyqNtOLQa8psXLYUPdkEzj5XxD7E5lLOQQ83okEbH4Ykky3fpjRCzl4pnLiiQ1FQbHfwlOEw5xBAAA) check all favours and see how everyConst and everyLet are not updated ```svelte {#if true} {@const everyAtConst = FLAVOUR_LIST.every(flavour => flavours.includes(flavour))}

everyAtConst={everyAtConst}

{const everyConst = FLAVOUR_LIST.every(flavour => flavours.includes(flavour))}

everyConst={everyConst}

{let everyLet = FLAVOUR_LIST.every(flavour => flavours.includes(flavour))}

everyLet={everyLet}

everyDerived={everyDerived}

flavours={flavours}

{#each FLAVOUR_LIST as flavour} {/each} {/if} ``` ### Logs ```shell ``` ### System Info ```shell svelte 5.56.4 vite 8.1.3 ``` ### Severity annoyance

title: $derived re-executes once per reading effect (memoization lost) when invalidated while another batch is pending (experimental.async)
author: billpeet

title: Memory leak: a $derived only read in untracked contexts (e.g. a $state initializer) is never disconnected from its dependencies on destroy
author: northfacejmb

title: spurious `state_referenced_locally` with declaration tags accessed inside an event handler
author: ottomated
body:
### Describe the bug

Svelte doesn't realize that an event handler is a closure for declaration tags:

```svelte
{const phrase = $derived('Hello, ' + name)}
<button onclick={() => {
  console.log(phrase);
           // ^ state_referenced_locally
}}>
  Print
</button>
```

### Reproduction

[repl](https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.56.4#H4sIAAAAAAAAA22PTUvEMBCG_8owCG2xbO-xLXjz6N16yDazGoyTkEy7Ssh_l3QRL16feb8mI-tPQoVP5JyHq4_OQEvGCpkOe7xYRwnVS0b5DlVXAfa_rscQTmknJ5WddaL_-OpZiCWhwjGt0QaZF17EkUCVwwR3SbRQ2xz1Tfew8Dj8KXm0HDaBs2Wjdu02mnI1FhiOc149J4HwHnU60gxFu5Npm-OpHhq4P5q6svB43kQ8g-fV2fVjym0H0wy5Lqo53tHJ-bf2llanlDI_R8syDjfrjD0KfQkqiRuV1x5FW3e1bFBdtEtUfgC8FujpVQEAAA)

title: Compiler ignores previous plugin's source map, results in wrong source map
author: K1DV5
body:
### Describe the bug

Hello, I'm working on a Vite plugin (wuchale) and use `magic-string` to transform Svelte code and generate the source map. Sometimes, the source maps that the browser sees are sometimes wrong, and I used `vite-plugin-inspect` to check the output of wuchale and it seems correct, but then Svelte's compiler messes it up after that.

### Reproduction

Since the reproduction can't work in the REPL, and since it is fairly small, I'll provide the files here.

Start from the `svelte` template in `npm create vite@latest`, and edit only these two files:

```js
// vite.config.js

import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import { svelte } from '@sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte'
import MagicString from 'magic-string'

// https://vite.dev/config/
export default defineConfig({
    plugins: [
        {
            name: "tst",
            transform(code, id) {
                if (!id.includes('App.svelte')) {
                    return
                }
                const m = new MagicString(code)
                m.appendRight(10, 'let leben = 42\n\n')
                console.log(m.toString())
                return {
                    code: m.toString(),
                    map: m.generateMap(),
                }
            },
        },
        svelte()
    ],
})
```

```svelte
<!-- App.svelte -->

<script>

console.error('helo')

</script>

Hello, see devtools
```

Then run it, go to devtools, and look at the location of the `console.error` message, it will be at the beginning of the script block, not at the actual location.

### Logs

```shell
Not relevant
```

### System Info

```shell
System:
    OS: Linux 6.6 AlmaLinux 10.2 (Lavender Lion)
    CPU: (8) x64 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1195G7 @ 2.90GHz
    Memory: 5.62 GB / 7.58 GB
    Container: Yes
    Shell: 5.2.26 - /bin/bash
  Binaries:
    Node: 24.14.1 - /usr/bin/node
    npm: 11.11.0 - /usr/bin/npm
    bun: 1.3.11 - /home/K1DV5/.local/bin/bun
  npmPackages:
    svelte: ^5.56.4 => 5.56.4
```

### Severity

annoyance

title: SSR compilation of `{let ...= $derived(...)}` can incorrectly use getter instead of actual value
author: Conduitry
body:
### Describe the bug Using `{let foo = $derived()}` can, in at least some cases, result in `foo` being exposed as a _function_ that returns the appropriate value, rather than as the value itself. Client-side rendering seems to be fine, and using `let foo = $derived(...);` in the `
{typeof dt}
``` SSR-compiles into ```js import 'svelte/internal/flags/async'; import * as $ from 'svelte/internal/server'; export default function App($$renderer) { let dt = $.derived(() => Date.parse("2026-10-01T00:00:00Z")); $$renderer.push(`
${$.escape(typeof dt())}
`); } ``` ### Logs ```shell ``` ### System Info ```shell System: OS: Linux 6.18 Ubuntu 26.04 LTS 26.04 LTS (Resolute Raccoon) CPU: (8) x64 Intel(R) Core(TM) Ultra 7 258V Memory: 11.68 GB / 15.39 GB Container: Yes Shell: 5.3.9 - /bin/bash Binaries: Node: 24.18.0 Yarn: 1.22.22 npm: 11.16.0 pnpm: 11.9.0 npmPackages: svelte: 5.56.4 => 5.56.4 ``` ### Severity annoyance

title: $derived on a class instance returns a stale value after the effect that incidentally owned it at construction is destroyed
author: reinhard-sanz
body:
### Describe the bug

A $derived declared as a class field is "owned" by whatever effect happens to be active when the instance is constructed (derived.parent = active_effect). If that instance outlives the effect, e.g. a long-lived model object that was new'd inside a component which later unmounts, the derived is frozen to its last value and never recomputes again, even though its $state dependencies keep changing.

This is a regression introduced in 5.55.3 by #17921; pinning to 5.55.2 restores correct behavior.

The trap is that the owning effect is non-deterministic and semantically meaningless for an object whose lifetime isn't tied to any component, it's just whichever effect was on the stack at new-time.



### Reproduction

https://svelte.dev/playground/86e9f769bb1d4021af59f2d289b73448?version=5.56.4

Click the button twice.
Expected: after step 2, box.value === 'C' so box.doubled === 'CC'.
Actual (5.55.3+): box.doubled === 'BB', frozen at the value from before the child unmounted. Reading it inside a reactive context (template/$effect) instead reconnects and unfreezes it, which makes the staleness order-dependent and hard to spot.


### Logs

```shell

```

### System Info

```shell
System:
    OS: macOS 26.4.1
    CPU: (14) arm64 Apple M4 Pro
    Memory: 3.37 GB / 48.00 GB
    Shell: 5.9 - /bin/zsh
  Binaries:
    Node: 22.18.0 - /Users/reinhard/.nvm/versions/node/v22.18.0/bin/node
    npm: 10.9.4 - /Users/reinhard/.nvm/versions/node/v22.18.0/bin/npm
    bun: 1.3.14 - /Users/reinhard/.bun/bin/bun
  Browsers:
    Brave Browser: 146.1.88.134
    Chrome: 149.0.7827.156
    Firefox: 152.0.3
    Safari: 26.4
  npmPackages:
    rollup: ^4.56.0 => 4.56.0
```

### Severity

blocking an upgrade

title: Transition in each not work.
author: raydylate

title: Error thrown by a $derived re-executing during teardown escapes <svelte:boundary> (and can mask the real error via null effect.b)
author: Stadly

title: Snippets: I think Svelte is still missing one capability
author: FredySandoval
body:
### Describe the problem

There is one feature that would blur this distinction nicely.

Imagine if snippets could accept attributes:

```svelte
{#snippet footer(class)}
    <footer class={`border-t p-4 ${class}`}>
        {@render children()}
    </footer>
{/snippet}
```




### Describe the proposed solution

Usage:

```svelte
<Card>
    {@render footer("justify-end gap-2")}
</Card>
```

or even something resembling:

```svelte
{#snippet footer class="justify-end gap-2"}
```


### Importance

nice to have

title: make $state.snapshot work in non svelte files
author: zhihengGet
body:
### Describe the problem

$state.snapshot can only be used in *.svelte.ts , idk if this is hard rule,  can we allow snapshot to be used  everywhere

so i dont have to rename

### Describe the proposed solution

export an `snapshot` fn or make $state.snapshot work anywhere,  

### Importance

nice to have

title: Provide hasContext function with createContext
author: soleuniverse101
body:
### Describe the problem

I find `createContext` really useful but it only provides `get`/`set` functions equivalent to `getContext` and `setContext`, while missing the `hasContext` function. This can be painful because there is no way to check if a context exists if the key is not available. Also trying to get it will throw an error.

### Describe the proposed solution

Providing a third function for `createContext` to return a `[get, set, has]` triplet.

### Importance

nice to have
